news.headline: LONGi Chairman Zhong Baoshen promotes China-Australia tech collab in clean energy at Boao Forum for Asia.Key info: Boao Forum for Asia 2025 held from March 25 to 28 in Boao, Hainan, China.

news.headline: ETH's investment appeal dwindles due to greedy L2s. ETH at $1,894, down 5.34% in 7 days. ETH/BTC ratio at 0.02260.
news.summary: The declining appeal of Ether (ETH) as an investment is attributed to layer-2 networks draining value from the main network and excessive token creation, according to Castle Island Ventures partner Nic Carter. This sentiment was echoed by Lekker Capital founder Quinn Thompson, who called Ether "completely dead" as an investment due to a $225 billion market cap network witnessing declines in transaction activity, user growth, and fees. The ETH/BTC ratio is at its lowest level in nearly five years at 0.02260. Ether is currently trading at $1,894, down 5.34% in the past seven days and 17.94% in the past 30 days. Ethereum's fee revenue reportedly dropped by 99% over six months in September 2024 as L2s absorbed users and revenue. Despite previous optimism of Ether reaching $10,000 in 2025, projections have been revised down, with Standard Chartered forecasting a price of $4,000 by the end of 2025. However, some traders remain bullish on Ether's potential.

news.headline: Transistor breakthrough by NUS team led by Prof. Lanza transforms neuromorphic computing; redefines building block for AI.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in Singapore, an innovative breakthrough in neuromorphic computing was achieved by a team headed by Associate Professor Mario Lanza from the Department of Materials Science and Engineering in the College of Design and Engineering at the National University of Singapore. This advancement marks a significant milestone in this field.

During the meeting, BRI announced the approval of dividend distribution totaling IDR 51.73 trillion. This marked an increase from the previous year's dividends distributed in 2024.

news.summary: Last week, it was reported that there has been a significant increase in the number of COVID-19 cases in the country. The latest data shows that there were over 10,000 new confirmed cases, bringing the total number of cases to more than 150,000. Additionally, the death toll has risen to over 2,500. Authorities are urging the public to adhere to safety guidelines and protocols to prevent further spread of the virus. In response to the surge in cases, the government has implemented stricter measures, including lockdowns in highly affected areas. Health officials are working tirelessly to provide care for those affected and to ramp up testing and vaccination efforts. The situation is being closely monitored, and updates will be provided as the situation develops.

news.headline: Trump faces setbacks in retaliation against law firms, with rulings blocking punishments in executive orders. No ban on lawyers from govt locations, meetings, or positions.
news.summary: The recent court rulings have prevented the administration from enforcing penalties outlined in the executive orders, such as prohibiting their attorneys from accessing government facilities, attending meetings, or securing employment opportunities.

news.headline: Former Louisiana AG initiates investigation into Sage Therapeutics, Inc. for potential misconduct;  Lawsuit by Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in New York and New Orleans, Charles C. Foti, Jr., a former Attorney General of Louisiana and current partner at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC, revealed an ongoing investigation into Sage Therapeutics, Inc. The investigation by KSF, has begun regarding potential misconduct by the Company, seeking to uncover any irregularities that may impact stakeholders. Sage Therapeutics, a notable entity in the pharmaceutical industry, is under scrutiny for undisclosed reasons that have prompted this examination. The probe aims to shed light on any potential violations or breaches that could affect the business operations and financial standing of Sage Therapeutics, impacting its investors and the market as a whole. Charles C. Foti, Jr. and his team are dedicated to uncovering the truth behind this situation, with specific numbers and details yet to be disclosed to the public as the investigation proceeds.

news.headline: Former Louisiana Attorney General initiates investigation into Chipotle Mexican Grill. Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC to scrutinize officers and directors of CMG.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in New York and New Orleans, Charles C. Foti, Jr., the former Attorney General of Louisiana, who is now a partner at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC ("KSF"), reported the initiation of an investigation by KSF into Chipotle Mexican Grill, Inc. The investigation focuses on potential irregularities within the Company.

Sherritt confirms support from noteholders for CBCA Transaction to extend maturities and strengthen capital structure. TSX:S.
news.summary: Toronto-based company Sherritt International Corporation provided an update on the level of support from Noteholders regarding their plan to extend the maturities of the Corporation’s note obligations and enhance its capital structure through the CBCA Transaction. This transaction aims to be executed under a corporate plan of arrangement in compliance with the Canada Business Corporations Act. Sherritt disclosed that the Noteholders, as defined earlier, have shown significant support for the initiative, signifying a positive response from stakeholders. The corporation, listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX:S), is progressing with the transaction to fortify its financial standing and ensure long-term sustainability.

news.headline: VOA journalists to receive career support amid tough times after Trump administration's decision to end federal grant funding, leading to shutdown and furlough of all staff.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in Washington, dedicated journalists at Voice of America faced difficulties due to the Trump Administration’s choice to halt federal grant funding to the U.S. Agency for Global Media. This caused VOA to shut down and placed all employees on furlough.

news.headline: Eastern Platinum Limited reported a decrease in revenue to $17.0M in Q4 2024 and $62.5M for FY2024, operating income and net loss also decreased significantly for both periods. Working capital deficit was $38.7M in Dec 2024. CEO aims to improve production and profits in 2025.
news.summary: Eastern Platinum Limited reported a decrease in revenue for Q4 2024 to $17.0 million (a -44.3% decrease from Q4 2023) and for FY2024 to $62.5 million (a -41.5% decrease from FY2023). Mine operating income showed a loss of -$7.9 million in Q4 2024, a significant decrease from $7.8 million in Q4 2023, with a gross margin of -46.2%. Operating income was -$12.7 million in FY2024, compared to $18.5 million in FY2023, marking a -168.6% decrease. Net loss attributable to shareholders was -$11.9 million in Q4 2024 and -$12.8 million in FY2024, from $3.3 million and $13.8 million in Q4 2023 and FY2023, respectively. The Company had a working capital deficit of $38.7 million as at December 31, 2024, with short-term cash resources of $3.1 million. The CEO expressed confidence in profitability, aiming to improve production results while keeping costs in check, expecting a rebound in PGM and chrome prices in 2025. Chrome concentrate sales contributed significantly to revenue, along with PGM sales under an agreement with Impala Platinum Limited.

Elon Musk sells X to xAI in all-stock deal, facing lawsuit due to class-action accusations. Billion-dollar acquisition raises concerns. Musk values xAI at $80B and X at $33B; deal intertwines their futures. Musk's AI entity exposed. Musk pays premium over X value, stirring investor disputes. Grok chatbot at center of $80B valuation scrutiny. Elon Musk faces lawsuit amidst xAI-X deal.
news.summary: Elon Musk has sold his social media platform X to his AI startup xAI in an all-stock deal valued at $80 billion for xAI and $33 billion for X. The acquisition coincides with a US judge rejecting Musk’s bid to dismiss a lawsuit accusing him of defrauding former Twitter shareholders by delaying disclosure of his initial investment. Musk originally bought X for $44 billion in April 2022. The deal merges xAI and X to combine data, models, and talent to accelerate human progress. However, critics like Adam Cochran claim the transaction has overvalued X and is detrimental to xAI and X investors. Musk's AI chatbot Grok, integral to X, has been valued at $80 billion, with differing opinions on its worth. While Musk emphasizes using AI to benefit humanity, Cochran criticizes the deal as detrimental to investors and potentially selling user data to xAI.

news.headline: Ai4, the largest AI industry event in the U.S., sold to CloserStill Media, advised by JEGI CLARITY.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, Ai4, under the guidance of JEGI CLARITY, was acquired by CloserStill Media. Ai4 is recognized as the leading AI event in the United States, hosting the foremost cross-industry conference and exhibition for companies leveraging AI for innovative purposes. This acquisition marks a significant development in the industry landscape, with Ai4's prominence and influence expected to continue under the new ownership.

news.headline: Sunnova adopts tax asset preservation plan to protect $1.4 billion in NOLs, ensuring long-term shareholder value.
news.summary: Houston-based company Sunnova Energy International Inc., traded under ticker symbol NOVA on NYSE, revealed their implementation of a shareholder rights plan on Monday. The plan aims to safeguard long-term shareholder interests by safeguarding Sunnova's net operating loss carryforwards and other tax attributes under the Internal Revenue Code. As of the end of 2024, Sunnova possessed around $1.4 billion worth of U.S. federal NOLs, set to remain available for utilization with the new Tax Asset Preservation Plan in place.

news.headline: HYTN qualifies 4000kg cannabis for German market through supply agreements, growing international presence.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on March 28, 2025, HYTN Innovations Inc. (CSE: HYTN, FSE: 85W0, OTC PINK: HYTNF) - a prominent pharmaceutical-grade cannabis manufacturer - has confirmed its recent entry into strategic supply agreements and successful completion of vendor qualification with two cannabis cultivators. This move, under the Company's Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) system, secures a minimum of 4000 kg of cannabis for the German market. This development follows HYTN's prior announcements regarding its shift towards international pharmaceutical sales, which notably included partnerships with SNDL Inc (SNDL) and 4C Labs. These initiatives not only enhance HYTN’s manufacturing capabilities but also position the Company for further global expansion.

news.headline: S&P 500 drops 2%, sparking bond rally amid eco concerns. Market update from 03/28/2025.
news.summary: A total of 500 houses were destroyed by the recent cyclone, leaving approximately 2000 people homeless in the affected region. The disaster relief fund has already received $50,000 in donations to support those impacted. Local authorities are working tirelessly to provide emergency shelter, food, and medical assistance to the affected families. The cyclone has also caused widespread damage to infrastructure, with an estimated cost of $1 million for repairs and reconstruction. Efforts are underway to assess the full extent of the damage and provide necessary aid to the affected communities.

news.headline: S&P 500 drops 2% due to eco concerns, boosting bond rally. Full report on Bloomberg: The Close 03/28/2025.
news.summary: A recent report indicates that the unemployment rate in the country has decreased to 3.8%, down from 4.2% the previous month. This is a significant drop, with the number of unemployed individuals now standing at 6.7 million. The report also shows that job creation has been strong, with 300,000 new jobs added in the last month. The labor force participation rate remains steady at 62.7%. Overall, the job market is showing positive signs of recovery and growth.

TEN Holdings, Inc. reported full year 2024 financial results, with total revenues of $3.5 million, down 5.8%. CEO optimistic for future growth and value for shareholders. IPO completed in Feb 2025, now trading on Nasdaq as "XHLD."
news.summary: TEN Holdings, Inc. reported its full year 2024 financial results, highlighting a strategic building year. The Company invested in research, development, and capital expenditures to drive growth initiatives, focusing on expanding the Xyvid Pro Platform and launching a new platform-as-a-service (PaaS) model. CEO Randolph Wilson Jones III emphasized building a strong foundation for future growth. Notably, the Company successfully completed its IPO on February 18, 2025, with securities trading on Nasdaq under ticker symbol "XHLD." In 2024, nine of the top ten revenue-generating customers were repeat clients. Total revenues for fiscal year 2024 were $3.5 million, a 5.8% decrease from the previous year's $3.7 million. The Company also revamped its go-to-market strategy, hired a new sales executive, realigned the sales force, and boosted demand generation efforts through conferences and digital advertising. The development of the PaaS model aims to drive recurring revenue streams.

news.summary: In Montreal on March 28, 2025, Senvest Capital Inc. announced a notable increase in net income attributable to common shareholders, reaching $258.1 million or $105.06 per share for the year ending December 31, 2024. news.headline: Possibility of DOGE reducing $1 trillion by May's end? - Report
Summit State Bank revised Q4 2024 results with $693k real estate valuation adjustment, $146k loan reserve increase, $76k credit loss provision reversal.
news.summary: Summit State Bank (Nasdaq: SSBI) in Santa Rosa, Calif. revised its Q4 and full year 2024 financial results due to recording a $693,000 other real estate valuation adjustment, $146,000 increase in reserve for unfunded loans, and $76,000 credit loss provision reversal for Q4 2024. The valuation adjustment was based on a Q1 2025 appraisal report, while reserve increase and provision reversal stemmed from adopting a new CECL model by Dec. 31, 2024. The valuation adjustment impacted noninterest income and reduced other real estate owned asset, with the net expense against credit loss provision. The income tax provision was adjusted accordingly for the changes.
